codeReview.summary: Feature implementation is complete and well-tested. The worker fixed a critical bug in iox_perror() where it was using hardcoded 0 instead of errno, and added comprehensive test coverage for VAL-LIBC-011 through VAL-LIBC-015. All three core API functions (iox_version, iox_strerror, iox_perror) are implemented correctly with thread-safe designs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly followed the iox_ prefix convention for all public API functions as documented in AGENTS.md naming conventions section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-libc/src/iox_version.c: All functions use iox_ prefix (iox_version, iox_strerror, iox_perror)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The errno handling in iox_strerror correctly maps positive errno values (Linux convention) to negative iox_error codes. This mapping logic is important for compatibility.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-libc/src/iox_version.c:50-53 - maps positive errnum to negative before array lookup

sharedStateObservations.observation: Thread safety is achieved through read-only data structures. The iox_error_strings array is static const, and iox_version() returns a constant string. No mutexes or synchronization primitives are needed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-libc/src/iox_version.c:22-53 - static const char array; line 58 - returns constant string

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test file demonstrates proper use of pthread for thread-safety testing, which is the project's established pattern for concurrency testing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-libc/tests/test_core_smoke.c: Uses pthread_create/pthread_join for 8 concurrent threads

summary: PASS: Feature libc-api-functions is complete and fully functional. Commit 88bec9d35 contains the fix for iox_perror() to use errno instead of hardcoded 0, and comprehensive tests covering VAL-LIBC-011 through VAL-LIBC-015. All three functions (iox_version, iox_strerror, iox_perror) work correctly and are thread-safe. Test coverage includes: version string format validation, strerror error code mapping (including positive errno values), perror output to stderr with prefix, and concurrent thread safety verification with 8 threads.
